summary: Set 052 Session 1 of 3 (Audit & design-lock). Mapped the cost path and re-diagnosed the dead-icon bug as a read/write path mismatch: the router writes ai_router/router-metrics.jsonl (metrics.log_filename, default) while the dashboard reads hardcoded ai_router/metrics.jsonl (utils/metrics.ts:5), so the dashboard is always empty regardless of the (already-default-ON) metrics.enabled flag; the placeholder's config.py METRICS_ENABLED flag is fictional. Found staleness infra already exists (metadata.pricing_reviewed + review_frequency_days + config.py:_check_pricing_staleness, currently firing at 40d>30d) and per-model rates live in router-config.yaml. Ran cross-provider consensus (gemini-2.5-pro + gpt-5.4) on the five open questions: agreement on four, genuine split on Q4 (tier gate) resolved to a router-capability gate (gpt-5.4 flagged that gemini's derive-from-session-sets re-creates the dead icon). Locked design D1-D8 in docs/proposals/2026-05-29-cost-metrics-icon/{proposal,verdict}.md (raw consensus in consensus-output.json) and reconciled spec.md. UAT elected by operator (suggestion_disposition: uat, session 1). Scope deltas for S2: NEW #1 deliverable = read-path fix; metrics-on-by-default dropped as a no-op; empty state -> three honest states (disabled/empty/data); tier gate -> router-capability. Routed cost $0.048427 (gemini-2.5-pro $0.006215 + gpt-5.4 $0.042213); cumulative Set 052 $0.048427 of $10 NTE.

reason.specifics: S2 (Implementation) continues the same Claude trajectory: a focused TS-mostly change set (read-path fix, router-capability tier gate, staleness banner, three honest states + tests) that benefits from the audit context already in this orchestrator's working memory. No blocker or cost reason to switch providers.
